channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Top Russian and US officials are set to hold talks on Ukraine war without Kyiv
item.description: Top Russian and U.S. officials are set to hold talks hold talks in Saudi Arabia in a bid to improve their ties and negotiate an end to the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Baraa Anwer And Dasha Litvinova, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Philippine Vice President Duterte asks Supreme Court to void her impeachment and block Senate trial
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte asked the Supreme Court on Tuesday to void her impeachment and block a Senate trial that could remove her from office. The House of Representatives, which is dominated by the allies of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Top Russian and American officials will hold talks on ending the Ukraine war without Kyiv
item.description: RIYADH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — Senior American and Russian officials, including the countries’ top diplomats, will hold talks on improving their ties and negotiating an end to the war in Ukraine, officials said Monday, in what would be the most significant meeting between the sides since Moscow's full-
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, JUSTIN SPIKE and BARRY HATTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hundreds of FAA workers laid off amid staffing shortages, raising safety concerns
item.description: Hundreds of probationary workers with the Federal Aviation Administration were laid off over the weekend, raising concerns about air traffic safety as the agency struggles to meet hiring goals amid increased air traffic.
item.dc:creator: Ben Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Trump stuns Europe with opening Ukraine-Russia gambit
item.description: European leaders are scrambling to respond to President Trump’s first moves to end Russia’s war in Ukraine, shocked to find themselves on the outside of high-stakes talks about the continent’s security and grappling with a potential retreat of U.S. forces from Europe.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: 8 injured after Delta plane flipped over during landing incident in Canada
item.description: A Delta Air Lines plane has flipped upon arrival at Toronto’s Pearson Airport and ended up on its roof, injuring 18 people.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rubio meets with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio held a meeting Monday with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman in Riyadh. The State Department issued a statement on Rubio's meeting with the Saudi crown prince, saying Rubio emphasized the necessity of a Gaza solution that strengthens regional security.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: Delta jet crashes at Canada's Toronto Pearson Airport; crews responding to plane flipped upside down
item.description: Officials confirmed to Fox News Digital that Delta plane crashed at Toronto Pearson Airport on Monday afternoon. The CRJ-900 jet, operating as Delta 4819, departed from Minneapolis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Argentine judge investigates fraud case against President Milei over crypto promotion
item.description: A judge in Argentina has been selected to investigate allegations of fraud against President Javier Milei for his brief promotion of a cryptocurrency that collapsed in value hours after it was launched last week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Border town's residents rebuild in south Lebanon as Hezbollah leader calls for Israeli withdrawal
item.description: Residents of a south Lebanon town are waiting to rebuild and fix their damaged homes as Israel is supposed to end its military presence north of its border under a U.S.-brokered deal.
item.dc:creator: Bassem Mroue, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukraine presses Europe to nominate representative for Russia talks
item.description: Ukraine is urging Europe to quickly select a representative for potential talks with the U.S. and Russia on ending Kyiv's war with Russia. “It should be a quickly made decision,” Igor Zhovkva, the deputy head of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky’s office, told Bloomberg.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: Mexico awaits new response from Google on dispute over Gulf of Mexico name before filing lawsuit
item.description: Mexico says it’s awaiting a new response from Google to its request that the tech company fully restore the name Gulf of Mexico to its Google Maps service before filing a lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Taiwan cheers removal of ‘we do not support Taiwan independence’ from State Department website
item.description: Taiwan expressed approval after the U.S. State Department removed a statement on its website that said it does not support the island's independence. The phrase “we do not support Taiwan independence” was removed from a department fact sheet last week. The document, which highlights U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Retired general: Putin will likely 'wait out' Trump term and take Ukraine
item.description: Retired U.S. Army Gen. Jack Keane said he suspects Russian President Vladimir Putin will wait for President Trump to leave office before trying to topple the government in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Pope remains in hospital to treat 'complex clinical' condition: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis’ respiratory tract infection is presenting a “complex clinical picture” that will require further hospitalization, the Vatican said Monday as concerns grew about the increasingly frail health of the 88-year-old pontiff.
item.dc:creator: By NICOLE WINFIELD Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Zelenskyy not yet signing US economic agreement 'short-sighted,' White House official says
item.description: A White House national security official said Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy "is being short-sighted" in not signing a rare earth minerals deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US leaders in Saudi Arabia for peace talks on Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he will not accept a deal without a seat at the negotiating table.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Here are some takeaways from the first month of Trump's Mideast diplomacy
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — When Hamas threatened to call off the planned release of three Israeli hostages last week, U.S. President Donald Trump stepped into the picture with an unexpected ultimatum.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli military experts weigh in on Trump's ‘all hell’ threat to Hamas and what it could look like
item.description: Former Israeli military officials discuss how President Donald Trump's threats against Hamas could play out in Gaza, and what strategies the IDF could adopt if they go back into the enclave.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australia plans to deport 3 violent criminals to Nauru
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia will soon deport three violent criminals including a convicted murderer to Nauru even though they are not citizens of the tiny Pacific atoll, after an Australian court ruled that dangerous immigrants can no longer be imprisoned indefinitely.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: European leaders want a say in talks to end Russia's war in Ukraine. The US is brushing them off
item.description: European leaders have insisted their governments must have a say in talks between the U.S. and Russia to end the war in Ukraine, as both Moscow and Washington said there was no role for Europe in the negotiations that could shape the future of the continent.
item.dc:creator: Raf Casert And Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Earth’s solid metal core may be soft on the outside: Study
item.description: Rather than being perfectly ball-shaped, the Earth's inner core may actually resemble peaks and valleys.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Rwanda-backed M23 rebels breach second major city in Congo's mineral-rich east
item.description: M23 rebels have reportedly "occupied" Bukavu, Democratic Republic of the Congo, positioning themselves at the governor's office and vowing to deviate from the "old regime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in fatal stabbing in Austria had an 'Islamic terror motive,' officials say
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — Austrian authorities said Sunday the stabbing of six people that left a 14-year-old boy dead was carried out by a man with possible connections to the Islamic State group who appeared to have acted alone.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LIECHTENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Austria stabbing attack suspect is Syrian migrant who pledged allegiance to Islamic State, officials say
item.description: Austrian officials said a Syrian migrant carried out an IS-motivated knife attack a day after Vice President JD Vance derided EU leaders on mass migration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Food delivery driver helped stop suspect following deadly stabbing: officials
item.description: A 23-year-old with possible connections to the Islamic State group is in custody after a stabbing of six passersby that left a 14-year-old boy dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Food delivery driver helped stop suspect following deadly Austria stabbing: officials
item.description: A 23-year-old with possible connections to the Islamic State group is in custody after a stabbing of six passersby that left a 14-year-old boy dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turkish opposition party delegation meets with Kurdish leader in Iraq as part of PKK peace efforts
item.description: BAGHDAD (AP) — A Turkish opposition party delegation arrived in Iraq’s semiautonomous Kurdish region Sunday against the backdrop of peace efforts between Ankara and a banned Kurdish separatist movement in Turkey.
item.dc:creator: QASSIM ABDUL-ZAHRA and ÇINAR KIPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: American detained in Russia identified as Kalob Wayne Byers
item.description: Kalob Wayne Byers was identified as the American citizen recently detained at an airport in Moscow, Russia, on a drug smuggling charge, media reports say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel releases Palestinian prisoners linked to suicide bombings, attempted murders
item.description: Palestinian prisoners linked to suicide bombings and attempted murders against Israelis are among the 369 released Saturday by Israel in exchange for hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis will remain in hospital, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis will remain in the hospital for treatment, the Vatican said. He was admitted on Friday after holding audiences due to respiratory issues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A driver plows into a demonstration in Munich, injuring 30 people
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver drove a car into a labor union demonstration in central Munich on Thursday, injuring 30 people including children, authorities said. Officials said it was believed to be an attack. The suspect, an Afghan asylum-seeker, was arrested.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, Modi meet at Oval Office as US weighs tariffs
item.description: Before speaking with Trump, Modi met separately with Tesla CEO Elon Musk, a Trump administration official.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Nearly everyone in the world breathes bad air. How to lower your risk
item.description: Polluted air, laden noxious gasses or tiny, invisible particles that burrow into human bodies, kills 7 million people prematurely every year, the U.N. health agency estimates.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas' threat to delay hostage release raises fears for ceasefire
item.description: The next handover of three hostages had been scheduled for Saturday, and families say time is running out for those still alive.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
